name: Features Bento Grid
description: An asymmetric bento-box feature grid framed as a long-form section. Mixed tile sizes (one large hero, mediums, smalls), tilt-on-hover micro-interactions, per-card accent tints, and optional per-tile metrics and bullet sub-lists carry the grid itself. Around the grid, an optional project meta strip, methodology footnote, pull-quote, outcome metrics band and closing CTA scaffold the section into a long-form feature page rather than a stand-alone widget.

descriptions.descriptive: An asymmetric grid where one large hero tile anchors the composition, surrounded by medium and small tiles in a varied bento arrangement. Mixed corner radii, per-card accent gradients in the corners, and spring-physics tilt on hover give the section a playful, alive quality. Tiles can carry an optional mono-caps eyebrow above the title, an optional bullet sub-list under the description, and an optional inline metric anchored to the bottom in a font-mono numeral, giving the grid the data weight of a comparison table without dropping the playful register. Around the grid, optional editorial bands frame the section: a 4-up project meta strip above the grid, a methodology footnote in mono small caps under it, a serif pull-quote on a primary border-left, and a dark outcome metrics band before the closing CTA. Stacks to a single column below the large breakpoint.
descriptions.usage: Reach for this section when you need to showcase 5–7 features or benefits with visual variety rather than a flat row. The size hierarchy lets you put the most important benefit in the large tile and supporting points around it; the optional meta strip, footnote, pull-quote and metrics band let it stand in for an entire feature page when the brief calls for editorial depth. Best paired with bold or energetic hero sections; avoid below editorial, calm openers where the playful tilt feels off-tone.
descriptions.audienceFit: Fits creator tools, indie studios, lifestyle DTC, edtech, fintech-for-consumers — anywhere the brand voice leans warm, energetic, and confidently un-corporate. Less suitable for legal, financial advisory, or heritage-luxury brands that need restraint.
